page: Amazon Web Services c5n.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8124M processor running at 3.4 GHz. This configuration provides 8 dedicated vCPUs across 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor includes a 24 MB L3 cache and supports AVX and AVX-512F vector extensions, alongside AES cryptography acceleration.

The instance is equipped with 21.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, yielding 2.62 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage, requiring network-attached storage solutions. Networking capabilities are a key feature of the c5n family, delivering a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place the instance mostly in the average tier. It achieves a single-core Geekbench score of 1167.0 and a multi-core score of 4460.0, which is categorized as weak. Memory bandwidth tests show 413.11 GB/sec for cached reads and 49.97 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. In application-specific tests, it handles static web serving at 6.37 GB/sec and processes 7.25 million Redis SET operations per second. However, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and medium-model LLM inference show weaker performance.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c5n.2xlarge offers a balanced profile for network-bound and compute-heavy workloads without the overhead of local storage. It is well-suited for workloads such as web serving, software compilation, database operations, and network-intensive applications that leverage its 10 Gbps baseline bandwidth.
description: The Amazon Web Services c5n.2xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance utilizing the Nitro hypervisor. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8124M processor running at 3.4 GHz, it offers 8 dedicated vCPUs (4 physical cores) and 21.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The instance lacks local storage but features a 10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, making it highly optimized for network-dependent tasks. Benchmark data indicates average performance across most metrics, including a Geekbench single-core score of 1167.0 and a static web serving throughput of 6.37 GB/sec, though multi-core scaling in intensive tasks like bzip3 decompression is relatively weak. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for network-heavy compute tasks, making it suitable for web serving, database operations, and software compilation.

tagline: A dedicated compute-optimized instance featuring high-frequency Intel processors and enhanced baseline network bandwidth for data-intensive workloads.

21.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 2.62 GB per core
10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput networking
No local storage, relying entirely on network-attached storage
